title: Thread Scheduling Lifecycle
description: A flowchart depicting the various states a thread can be in and the transitions between them, managed by the operating system scheduler.
relationships: A thread is created and enters the New state
The scheduler admits a New thread, moving it to the Ready queue
The scheduler dispatches a Ready thread, moving it to the Running state
A Running thread requests a resource (e.g., I/O) and moves to Waiting
A Waiting thread receives its resource and returns to Ready
A Running thread completes its time quantum and is interrupted back to Ready
A Running thread finishes execution and moves to Terminated
